chapter_title.ar: بَابُ النَّفْرِ مِنْ مِنًى الْأَوَّلِ وَ الْآخِرِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 198 – The departure from Mina, the first ones and the later ones

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/215)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/215)
text: كَانَ أَبِي يَقُولُ لَوْ كَانَ لِي طَرِيقٌ إِلَى مَنْزِلِي مِنْ مِنًى مَا دَخَلْتُ مَكَّةَ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Mansour Bin Al Abbas, from Ali Bin Asbaat, from Suleyman Bin Abu Zaynab, from Is’haq Bin Ammar,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘My<sup>asws</sup> father<sup>asws</sup> was saying: ‘If there was a road for me<sup>asws</sup> to my<sup>asws</sup> house from Mina, I<sup>asws</sup> would not enter Makkah’.
en.sarwar: 9. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from Mansur ibn al-‘Abbas from Ali ibn Asbat from Sulayman ibn abu Zaynabah from Ishaq ibn ‘Ammar who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘My father, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, would say, “Had there been a road to my home from Mina’ I would not enter Makkah.’””
